description:
After fixing the libfuse stack overrun via 4vl.7's patches, our daemon survives ~10,000-12,000 FUSE ops during a buck2 build without crashing. But FSKit then force-unmounts the volume mid-build: fuse_loop returns cleanly (DESTROY received), our daemon shuts down via fuse_destroy, mount disappears. Buck2 sees EIO on whatever op it was issuing at the moment of unmount.

Daemon-side counters at unmount (one representative run):
  GETATTR  7322
  LOOKUP   ~6000
  STATFS   1226
  MKDIR     634
  CREATE    193
  WRITE     140
  SYMLINK   278
  FORGET    728  <-- only 728 FORGETs vs ~13K LOOKUP+GETATTR

That FORGET ratio means the daemon (and the FSKit appex it backs) is holding on to ~12K active nodeids in its tables. If macFUSE's FSKit appex has a per-volume nodeid table with a fixed cap or a memory budget, that's the most likely cause. /var/log shows nothing diagnostic.

Suspicions / things to try:

  1. Cap nodeid allocation aggressively — return high entry_timeout
     so the kernel can drop entries faster. Already at 300s; try 0 or 1
     for transient buck-out paths.
  2. Implement a more aggressive fuse_lowlevel_notify_inval_entry from
     our daemon when files are removed, to encourage FORGET.
  3. Build a minimal C repro (no Rust, no Buck2 — just a script that
     does mkdir/symlink/unlink in a loop) and see how many ops trigger
     the unmount. If it's reproducible cleanly, file upstream.
  4. Inspect the FSKit appex code (we can sip-strip it from the .appex
     bundle) to see if there's a hard limit.
  5. Try the macfuse-local appex variant instead of macfuse.
title: macFUSE FSKit unmounts daemon after ~10K ops during buck2 build
